mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 220i engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 220i engine typically costs £1,700–£4,200 supply only depending on which generation (N20 or B48), with used units available from £1,200–£2,200. Fitting adds £1,000–£2,000. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends entirely on which generation. The N20 (2014–2019) has a well-documented timing chain weakness — pre-2015 cars are high-risk; post-2015 cars are improved. The B48 (2017–present) is significantly more reliable, with only the oil filter housing and cooling system as known weaknesses. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 220i?

acceptedAnswer.text: N20: timing chain wear (pre-2015), valve cover and oil filter housing oil leaks. B48: oil filter housing cracking, cooling system leaks (water pump and hoses). The B48 is significantly more reliable than the N20.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 220i engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and generation. Against a well-kept £10,000 G42 220i, a £2,500–£4,200 recon is 25–40% of the car's value — worth it. Against a tired £3,000 F22 example with an N20, the same recon exceeds our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 220i engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: F22/F23 (2014–2019) and early F45 are N20B20; F44, G42, U06, and late F45 are B48A20. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N20 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: It needs replacement — the chain guides break and the tensioner wears. A full timing chain, tensioner, guide and VANOS sprocket replacement costs £1,200–£2,500. It's a labour-intensive job but far cheaper than a full engine replacement (£1,700+). The revised 2015+ components are the correct fix. At the first sign of cold-start rattle on an N20, book an inspection immediately.

hero.h1: BMW 220i Engine Replacement — Compare Prices & Get Quotes
hero.subHeadline: The 220i badge spans two entirely different engine families — the N20 (RWD-era) and the B48 (modern modular engine). Each has its own reliability profile, costs, and common failures. Get quotes from UK specialists for reconditioned, rebuilt and used units today.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current 220i market. N20-engined cars sit at the lower end; B48 examples command a premium. See the Era Map above for generation-specific context.

problems.title: N20 Timing Chain and Guide Failure (Pre-2015)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start lasting 2–5 seconds, metallic ticking from the front of the engine, engine warning light, cam timing errors, eventual chain slip
problems.repairCost: £1,200–£2,500 (timing chain, tensioner, guide and VANOS sprocket replacement)
problems.replacementCost: £1,700–£3,000 (if chain jumps and causes valve interference)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: any cold-start rattle on a pre-2015 N20 requires immediate investigation

problems.recommendation: The N20's timing chain failure is well-documented. BMW faced a class action lawsuit in the US over this issue. The plastic chain guides degrade and can break, sending debris into the oil pickup. The fix is to replace the entire timing chain system with the revised 2015+ components. If you're buying a pre-2015 N20-engined 220i and there's no proof this work has been done, budget for it immediately — or walk away. Frequent oil changes (every 5,000 miles) are the key to avoiding this issue.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: N20 Oil Leaks (Valve Cover and Oil Filter Housing)
problems.symptoms: Burning oil smell, visible oil spots under the car, low oil level warning, smoke from the engine bay
problems.repairCost: £500–£1,200 (valve cover gasket or oil filter housing gasket replacement)
problems.recommendation: The N20 is known for oil leaks from the valve cover gasket and oil filter housing gasket. These are well-understood jobs, but the oil filter housing gasket requires dismantling the intake manifold. If left unchecked, oil leaks can drip onto the exhaust manifold, causing smoke and a burning smell, or lead to low oil levels and engine damage. Catch them early.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil leak guide

problems.title: B48 Oil Filter Housing and Cooling System Leaks
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, oil spots under the car, low oil pressure warning, engine warning light, sweet smell from the engine bay
problems.repairCost: £800–£1,800 (oil filter housing replacement, including intake manifold removal)
problems.recommendation: The B48's oil filter housing is a known weak point. The unit can crack, causing coolant or oil leaks. Because it sits under the intake manifold, replacement is labour-intensive. This is the B48's most common issue — it affects everything from the 2 Series to the Supra. Check for coolant loss or oil spots regularly, and investigate any drop in fluid levels promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full oil filter housing failure guide

problems.title: B48 Cooling System Leaks (Water Pump and Hoses)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss, rising temperature gauge, warning light, steam from the engine bay, heater not working properly
problems.recommendation: The B48's electric water pump and coolant return line hoses are known to fail. Owners report that coolant hose failure is "not a matter of if they will fail, but when". These are typically 60,000–100,000 mile items. Replacing the water pump and hoses preventatively at around 70,000 miles is cheaper than dealing with an overheated engine.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

rows.whenItMakesSense: The most important upgrade — far cheaper than a full engine
If chain jumps and valves hit pistons, replacement is the only option
Straightforward replacement — catch before oil starvation
Labour-intensive but well-understood; once replaced, issue rarely recurs

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 220i? Check These First
buyIf: * B48-engined car (2017+ on Active Tourer, 2020+ on Gran Coupé, 2021+ on G42) — the more reliable engine
* N20-engined car with documented timing chain work using 2015+ revised components — the single most important check
* Full service history with correct BMW-spec oil (5W-30 LL-01 or 0W-20)
* Evidence of regular maintenance and quality fuel
avoidIf: * N20-engined car (pre-2015) with no proof of timing chain work — you're buying a ticking time bomb
* Any cold-start rattle — this is almost certainly timing chain tensioner wear on N20
* Evidence of oil leaks or coolant loss — indicates common N20 or B48 issues
* Poor service history — both engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 220i badge tells a tale of two engines. The N20 (2014–2019) is a capable turbo four-cylinder with a well-documented timing chain weakness — pre-2015 cars are high-risk, post-2015 cars are improved. The B48 (2017–present) is significantly more reliable, with well-understood oil filter housing and cooling system weaknesses. Buy the B48 if you can — it's the modern, dependable choice. If you're buying an N20, demand timing chain history.

cta.label: → Compare 220i engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 220i (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Cold Start — Listen for rattles lasting more than 1–2 seconds (timing chain warning on N20)
2. Service History — including which engine era (N20 or B48) and evidence of timing chain work on N20
3. Oil Spots — Any oil under the car? (valve cover or oil filter housing leak warning)
4. Coolant Level — Any unexplained loss? (B48 oil filter housing or cooling system warning)
5. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
6. Turbo Performance — Test acceleration; any hesitation or whistling?
7. Oil Level — Check for excessive consumption (N20 rings can be an issue)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (catalyst, O2 sensors)
